description: Transparency-log consistency-proof golden vector (RFC-ACDP-0012 §8.2 consistency mode, §9.2). Over the log-001 five-leaf tree: the RFC 6962 §2.1.2 consistency proof PROOF(3, D[5]) between tree sizes 3 and 5, with BOTH checkpoints signed by the registry test keypair. A verifier holding the retained size-3 checkpoint and the fresh size-5 checkpoint MUST reproduce the §9.2 verification end-to-end — this is the history-rewrite detector, the reason the log exists. Executed arithmetically by scripts/conformance-runner.py. Values generated by temp/gen-0.3.0-vectors.py.

registry_test_keypair.warning: TEST ONLY. The private seed is publicly known (identical to rcpt-001's). Never use this keypair for real checkpoints.

vectors.name: Consistency proof between tree sizes 3 and 5 of the log-001 tree, both checkpoints signed
verification_steps: 1. Recompute MTH over leaf_hashes[0:3] and leaf_hashes[0:5] (RFC-ACDP-0012 §5.2); the roots MUST equal expected.first_root_hash and expected.second_root_hash.

2. JCS-canonicalize both checkpoint_unsigned objects; results MUST equal the expected canonical forms; the checkpoint hashes MUST match; signing the ASCII bytes of each checkpoint hash with the registry test key MUST reproduce the pinned signatures, and both MUST verify under the registry test public key (RFC-ACDP-0012 §6, §9.3).

3. Recompute RFC 6962 §2.1.2 PROOF(3, D[5]) from the leaf hashes; it MUST equal expected.consistency_proof_response.consistency_path (four elements: leaf hash 2, leaf hash 3, node(leaf0,leaf1), leaf hash 4).

4. Run the RFC-ACDP-0012 §9.2 verification algorithm with first=3, second=5, the pinned path, and the two pinned roots; it MUST succeed. Tampering any path element, either root, or either size MUST make it fail (invalid_log_proof — see log-002).
